commands.review_summary:
The PR adds artifact-coverage tracking to prompt-backed ClawScan evaluation, floors incomplete prompt coverage out of clean results, persists the coverage signal, and updates schema, package API typing, tests, and security-moderation specs.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main truncates prompt-backed artifact text without persisting a coverage signal or flooring benign results, and the PR tests cover oversized SKILL.md and omitted-content cases. I did not run a live current-main reproduction because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real dry-run proof and maintainer acceptance of the scanner trust-boundary semantics; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue right now.

target.title: fix: floor incomplete ClawScan coverage
target.body:
## What this PR does

This PR makes the prompt-backed ClawScan evaluator fail closed when ClawHub did not review the full material artifact text.

## Scenario this protects

The legacy/local evaluator builds a prompt from `SKILL.md` and uploaded file contents. That prompt path has size limits.

If `SKILL.md` is truncated, file contents are truncated, or later file blocks are omitted after the aggregate prompt limit, a benign model answer should not be stored as clean. In that situation, there is still unreviewed artifact text that may matter.

## What changed

- Adds an explicit artifact coverage signal for the prompt-backed evaluator path.
- Records incomplete coverage when prompt assembly truncates `SKILL.md`.
- Records incomplete coverage when prompt assembly truncates file content.
- Records incomplete coverage when file blocks are omitted after the aggregate prompt limit.
- Records incomplete coverage when hidden comments are stripped before review.
- Converts benign answers from incomplete coverage into suspicious/review results before storage.
- Keeps incomplete coverage visible in moderation with `review.artifact_incomplete`.

## What maintainers should expect

A clean-looking result from incomplete prompt coverage can no longer silently clear review state. The caller gets a review-floor result instead, with the reason preserved.

The modern GitHub Actions Codex worker path is different: it materializes artifact files into a workspace for Codex instead of relying on the sliced prompt text. This PR still preserves parsed `artifactCoverage` if that parser path receives it.

SkillSpector and static scan findings remain advisory/context inputs. They do not replace the ClawScan verdict, so the review floor belongs at the LLM result/moderation boundary.

## Validation

- `bun test convex/lib/securityPrompt.test.ts scripts/local-clawscan-dry-run.test.ts convex/lib/moderationEngine.test.ts`
- `bunx vitest run convex/securityScan.test.ts convex/llmEval.test.ts scripts/security/run-codex-scan-worker.test.ts`
- `bunx convex codegen`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/schema/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bunx tsc -p packages/clawhub/tsconfig.json --noEmit`
- `bun run ci:static`
- `bun run ci:unit`
- `bun run ci:types-build`
- `bun run ci:packages`

`bun test convex/llmEval.test.ts convex/securityScan.test.ts scripts/security/run-codex-scan-worker.test.ts` was also tried, but `convex/securityScan.test.ts` requires Vitest APIs such as `vi.stubEnv` and `vi.mocked`; the same files passed under Vitest as listed above.
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski

## What changed

- Adds an explicit artifact coverage signal for the prompt-backed evaluator path.
- Records incomplete coverage when prompt assembly truncates `SKILL.md`.

- Records incomplete coverage when file blocks are omitted after the aggregate prompt limit.
- Records incomplete coverage when hidden comments are stripped before review.
